title: Multiline function parameters align their name-type colons
summary: Concrete parameters on separate rows share one absolute visible colon column determined by the widest rendered binding prefix in their parameter list.
details: Alignment is scoped to one multiline Tree-sitter parameter_list and never crosses function boundaries.
The target is an absolute display cell, so differing source indentation is included rather than assuming equal byte columns.
Alignment requires at most one complete parameter per source row; a row containing several parameters is fully flipped in compact form with no vertical padding for that list.
Pointer-object const prefixes such as const fixed participate in the visible binding width; auto-reference parameters have no colon and do not participate.
Padding is inline virtual text inserted immediately before the rendered colon. No source byte or source-row identity changes.
The list-level target is cached by buffer changedtick and style profile so repainting P parameter rows computes the list in O(P), not O(P squared).
Cursor and Visual reveal still show every row in its unaligned raw C++ form and restore the column immediately on exit.
rationale: A stable colon column makes name-first multiline signatures scan as one table and was the owner's previously intended layout.
Aligning presentation rather than source leaves clang-format policy, debugger rows, diffs, and compiler input untouched.
alternatives: Flip each parameter independently with the colon immediately after its name; rejected because long lists lose the intended table structure.
Rewrite source whitespace to align the transformed grammar; prohibited by the presentation-only invariant.

provenance.note: Owner identified missing colon alignment as inconsistent with the intended multiline function view.
Tree-sitter list analysis and changedtick/profile cache implement the accepted layout.

evidence.claim: Every colon shares a display column and Visual block reveal/restore preserves source bytes.

evidence.reference: style_lab/catalog.json qnpeps-layout scene and real ext-linegrid capture
evidence.claim: The browser oracle shows the aligned columns produced by actual Neovim.

questions: Should colons align across multiline function arguments?
Does parameter alignment alter source whitespace or line numbers?
How is multiline colon alignment cached?

last_verified.method: Owner confirmation followed by one-per-row and packed-row interaction assertions plus actual ext-linegrid capture of the reported signature shapes.
